text.title: Mobile Responsive Antipatterns
text.summary: Common failures in agent-generated responsive CSS: fixed widths, desktop-first, tiny touch targets, viewport unit bugs.

antipattern.description: Using fixed px widths for layout elements (width: 960px, width: 400px).
antipattern.reason: Fixed widths overflow on smaller viewports. Use max-width with percentage or fluid units instead. Exception: 1px borders and icon sizes.

antipattern.description: Writing desktop-first CSS using max-width media queries for mobile overrides.
antipattern.reason: Desktop-first produces cascading overrides that become unmaintainable. Every mobile style must undo a desktop style. Mobile-first is additive and produces less CSS.

antipattern.description: Using device-specific breakpoints (480px for iPhone, 1024px for iPad) instead of content-based breakpoints.
antipattern.reason: Device dimensions change every year. Break when the content breaks, not at specific device sizes.

antipattern.description: Interactive elements smaller than 44x44px — common when translating Figma designs pixel-for-pixel.
antipattern.reason: Touch targets below WCAG 2.5.5 minimum cause mis-taps. Figma designs often show visual size, not tap area. Add padding to meet the minimum.

antipattern.description: Hover-only interactions without active/focus equivalents for touch.
antipattern.reason: Touch devices have no hover state. Tooltips, dropdowns, and effects triggered only on :hover are invisible to mobile users.

antipattern.description: Using 100vh for full-height layouts on mobile.
antipattern.reason: On iOS Safari, 100vh includes the URL bar height, cutting off bottom UI. Use 100dvh (dynamic viewport height) or min(100vh, 100dvh) for older browser fallback.

antipattern.description: Missing initial-scale=1.0 in the viewport meta tag.
antipattern.reason: Without initial-scale, iOS Safari does not respect the viewport on rotation. Both width=device-width and initial-scale=1.0 are required.
